title: Cardiac Rhythm Recognition and Interpretation
catalogDescription: Covers advanced anatomy and physiology of the cardiovascular system and the role of the Cardiac Monitor/Electrocardiography (EKG) Technician in the patient setting. Includes overview of normal and abnormal rhythms of the heart with an emphasis on interpretation of electrocardiography in a dynamic healthcare environment and recognition of cardiac rhythms that require immediate intervention.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Cardiac Monitoring Lab
catalogDescription: Provides the skills and techniques for EKG and telemetry monitoring, interpretation, and documentation in the clinical setting. Includes review of normal and abnormal rhythms of the heart with an emphasis on interpretation of electrocardiography and recognition of cardiac rhythm that require immediate intervention.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Instructor consent.

title: Cardiac Monitoring Clinical
catalogDescription: This course provides students with the practical skills and clinical experience necessary to become proficient in the monitoring and interpretation of cardiac rhythms. Students will apply knowledge obtained in their theory and laboratory courses in a real-world healthcare setting to foster safe and effective clinical skills that support the healthcare team.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Instructor permission
